user_query:
Please manage our shared family grocery list using the data under input/grocery_data/. Assume my stored name is Abhishek and I’m the admin. Do not modify anything under input/; instead, write all updated files to output/grocery_data/ and produce the requested views under output/.

Tasks:
1) Add a new store: Target at 789 Elm Rd, Anytown with hours "8am–10pm daily".
2) Set Target as the primary store.
3) Set fallback order to: Target → Costco → Whole Foods.
4) Update category mappings: dairy → Whole Foods, produce → Whole Foods, electronics → Target.
5) Update Whole Foods hours to: "Mon–Sun 8am–9pm".
6) Add milk (2L) without specifying a store. It should resolve to Whole Foods due to the dairy category mapping.
7) Add 6 more eggs. If eggs are already on the list, detect the duplicate and merge quantities (x12 + x6 → x18). Log the merge in history.
8) Remove olive oil from the list (it’s currently at Costco). Log the removal in history.
9) Show a grouped, nicely formatted list that includes each store’s name, full address, and hours in the header, plus an "📋 Unassigned" section for unassigned items. End with "Total items: [count]".

Outputs to create:
- Write updated output/grocery_data/config.json, output/grocery_data/list.md, and output/grocery_data/history.md reflecting the changes above. Keep users as-is.
- Write output/view_list.txt containing the rendered list exactly as you would display it (include addresses and hours in each store heading and the total item count at the end).
- Write output/summary.md summarizing what you changed (e.g., primary store, fallback order, category mappings, hours updates, duplicate merge, and removals).

Use only the provided files under input/grocery_data/ as the shared storage reference. If you would normally verify store hours or item availability via web search, skip it and use the explicit hours and stores I’ve provided. Attribute all writes with my name and an ISO timestamp in list.md and history.md. Always group items by store when writing the view, and include the total item count across all stores and the unassigned section.
